name: Lamb Klephtiko (Weir)
recipeIngredient: OR 5-to-6 pounds total -- boned and trimmed with excess fat
recipeInstructions:
PREP: Grind bay leaves to a powder in a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Preheat the oven to 200°F. Combine the sliced garlic, ground bay leaves,
cinnamon, oregano, salt, and pepper in a small bowl; stir well to blend.
Using a sharp paring knife, make 1-inch incisions in the lamb; insert the
garlic slices and herb mixture into the incisions.
Roll up the lamb and tie it securely with kitchen string.
Rub the outside of the roast with oil, then season with salt and pepper.
Wrap the lamb tightly in parchment paper, sealing all edges well to prevent
steam from escaping.
Place the package on a rack in a roasting pan. Add water to the pan to come
almost up to the level of the rack but do not touch the parchment package.
Bake for 4 hours, adding more water if necessary.
Remove the package and transfer the roast to a heated platter. Drizzle with
the juices from the package. Serve with Tzatziki, if desired.
Serves 8.
[PER SERVING: 320 calories, 47 g protein, 1 g carbohydrate, 14 g fat (4 g
saturated), 144 mg cholesterol, 145 mg sodium, 0 g fiber.]
Notes: A whole leg of lamb, boned and trimmed with excess fat, is infused
with a mixture of ground bay leaves, garlic, cinnamon, and oregano, then
wrapped in parchment paper and baked slowly for 4 hours until perfectly
done and juicy. Originally known as klephtiko, it became a traditional way
of cooking fish or meat, especially lamb. Recipes from Stoyanof's Greek
neighborhood taverna in San Francisco. Joanne Weir, "Cherished Greek Dish
in a Plain Paper Wrapper," San Francisco Chronicle, June 11, 1997.]
